description_long: This is a Roman-style military standard featuring a vertical wooden pole topped with a black metallic spearhead finial and a matching pointed base. The central feature is a rectangular red banner adorned with a golden eagle with outstretched wings, clutching a laurel wreath, and the letters 'S.P.Q.R.' beneath, flanked by decorative gold borders and two vertical red streamers with gold trim. The pole appears sturdy and is likely made of wood, while the finial and base are metallic, providing both decorative and functional reinforcement. The banner is fabric, tightly attached to a horizontal crossbar at the top of the pole, ensuring it hangs flat and visible. The overall design is ceremonial and symbolic, evoking Roman authority and tradition, and would be used in parades, historical reenactments, or as a decorative urban object. The colors are vivid, and the object appears well-maintained and visually striking.
description: A Roman-style standard with a red banner, golden eagle, and 'S.P.Q.R.' text, mounted on a wooden pole with black metallic spearhead finials. The design is ceremonial, detailed, and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full red banner with golden eagle, 'S.P.Q.R.' text, and decorative streamers.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pole and the minimal visibility of the banner.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, with the banner's reverse side and pole details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the pole's thin profile and the edge of the banner.
attribute_graphic_design: The banner features a large golden eagle with outstretched wings, a laurel wreath, and the text 'S.P.Q.R.' in gold. Decorative gold borders and two vertical red streamers with gold trim flank the main design.
